cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the order of the books on the shelf. There are five books: white, gray, purple, yellow, and black. The order is fixed, and we need to determine their positions based on the given clues. First, let me list out the clues again to make sure I have them all: 1. The yellow book is to the left of the white book. 2. The black book is to the left of the yellow book. 3. The purple book is the rightmost (so it's in the fifth position). 4. The white book is to the left of the gray book. Let me try to arrange the books from left (position 1) to right (position 5). I know the purple book is in position 5. From clue 2, the black book is to the left of the yellow book, so black < yellow. From clue 1, the yellow book is to the left of the white book, so yellow < white. From clue 4, the white book is to the left of the gray book, so white < gray. So combining these: black < yellow < white < gray < purple. Let me test this order.
